opportunities.entry_trigger: Scale into longs $65,500-$68,000 on intraday dips toward the demand zone; require a daily close above $65,000 to hold. Add on any 4H close back above $70,000.
opportunities.rationale: BTC closed ~$67.9K on Mar 31 after oscillating between mid-60Ks and high-60Ks throughout late March. Old entries (last_updated Mar 22) are stale (>1.5x holding window); this is a fresh setup. The $65K-$68K demand zone held the prior correction repeatedly. Crypto sentiment in Extreme Fear band: historically contrarian. Spot ETF inflows and war/stagflation backdrop supportive medium-term. R:R from $66.8K midpoint to $76K vs $61.5K stop ~1:2.0. Invalidation: daily close below $61,500. Next major catalyst: NFP Apr 4.
data_sources: TwelveData BTC/USD Coinbase historical data Mar 2026

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter $1,950-$2,050 only if BTC holds above $65,000; invalidated on any 4H close below $1,900. Prefer limit orders near $1,980-$2,000 round-number support.
opportunities.rationale: ETH traded near $2,070 end of March, holding above the $1,900-$2,000 structural support band. ETH has underperformed BTC on ETH/BTC ratio during recent risk-off legs — higher-beta, not defensive. In high-vol Extreme Fear regime, altcoins must be sized small (1% risk max) and keyed off BTC support. R:R from $2,000 midpoint to $2,350 vs $1,780 stop ~1:1.8. Invalidation: daily close below $1,780.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter $80-$85 only if BTC holds above $65,000 and SOL prints a bullish RSI divergence on 4H. Exit immediately on BTC breakdown below $65K.
opportunities.rationale: SOL closed near $82.4 on Mar 31 after a choppy month (high ~$94, lows ~$80), keeping it in a broad $80-$95 trading range. Local lows have coincided with BTC testing high-60Ks support. Highest-beta of the three; capped at LOW conviction with 1% risk. R:R from $82.5 midpoint to $100 vs $74 stop ~1:2.1. Invalidation: daily close below $74 (Jan 2026 swing area).
data_sources: TwelveData SOL/USD Binance historical data Mar 2026
